title: Handlers and notify
description: Handlers are deferred tasks that run once at play end, only if a notifying task reported changed.
body: Use when coordinating service restarts after config changes. A handler is registered by name and triggered via notify; Ansible defers it to the end of the play and runs it once, only if at least one notifying task reported changed. Do not put a restart in a normal task if it should only happen on change. Actions: notify handlers from config tasks and rely on the deferred, deduplicated execution. Validation signals include a handler firing only on changed runs. Risk boundary: handlers are skipped if the play fails before the handler flush, so a critical restart may not happen — flush explicitly when needed.
